title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Go Client Sy… · Execution Trace: Client Configuration
summary: A reusable Go SDK needs one configured Client, narrow RoundTripper seams, ordered middleware, typed operations, and rotating configuration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go client configuration increment in a Go 1.26 net/http small composable client library boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Checkpoint traversal state, persist stable identities, resume after failure, and deduplicate without losing changed records. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
